archetype.name: Trust-Based Advisory
archetype.description: Advisory services — financial planning, legal counsel, wealth management, insurance advisory, tax strategy — where credentials, fiduciary duty, and outcome risk dominate.
archetype.promptEmphasis: Buyers enter via life events; evaluation and validation dominate the funnel; acquisition is slow and relational.

declarative: What does a {topicName} advisor actually do, and where does their authority come from?
How is {attribute1} different from {attribute2} when evaluating a {category} advisor?
What does fiduciary duty mean in {category} and when is it legally binding?
How are fees structured across {category} and which structures align incentives?
What are the standard credentials to look for in {category}?
comparative: What's the difference between {attribute1} and {attribute2} credentials in {category}?
Fee-only vs commission-based {category} advisors — what are you really paying for?
{attribute1} licensing vs {attribute2} licensing — which actually protects me legally?
RIA vs broker-dealer for {category} — who owes me what?
